next_steps: Perform dengue NS1 antigen and serology (IgM/IgG)
Send peripheral blood smear for malaria parasites (thick and thin films)
Order viral PCR panel for arboviruses and cardiac pathogens
Perform echocardiogram with strain analysis to evaluate acute myocarditis
Obtain diagnostic paracentesis for cell count, differential, and culture
Continue broad-spectrum antibiotics and vasopressor support
Monitor serial troponins and inflammatory markers

differential_diagnoses: Septic shock (secondary to spontaneous bacterial peritonitis or enteric translocation)
Acute viral myocarditis (likely arboviral, e.g., Dengue)
next_steps: Perform diagnostic paracentesis (cell count, differential, culture, albumin)
Obtain peripheral blood smear (thick/thin films) to confirm or exclude malaria
Order comprehensive arboviral testing (Dengue NS1, IgM/IgG, Chikungunya, Zika, Yellow Fever, Oropouche)
Obtain inflammatory markers (Ferritin, triglycerides, fibrinogen, soluble CD25) to screen for HLH
Continue intensive hemodynamic support and broad-spectrum antibiotic coverage
Repeat echocardiography to reassess myocardial function and evaluate for pericardial effusion evolution
Order serial labs: CBC with differential (look for atypical lymphocytes), CMP (liver function, renal function), and cardiac markers (troponin I/T, BNP)
